## 1. AnnualLeaveTrackerApp.swift

### Purpose
The app entry point. Decorated with `@main`, this struct bootstraps the SwiftUI app lifecycle. It reads the `hasCompletedSetup` flag and the `activeProfile` key from `AppStorage` to decide state, then unconditionally presents `SplashScreenView` as the root scene. The struct's name (`MyWorkDiaryApp`) differs from the filename — a cosmetic rename left in place.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None defined. Only the `App` conformance struct.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hasCompletedSetup` | Bool | `false` | Gates onboarding vs main app |
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Which of the two profiles is active |

> **Note:** `hasCompletedSetup` is read here but the conditional routing logic (show onboarding vs main content) is handled inside `SplashScreenView` / `ContentView`, not at this level. These values are loaded here merely to be available early.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- Renders `SplashScreenView()` as the single root view.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- None at this layer.

### UI Components Rendered
- `WindowGroup` → `SplashScreenView`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — Direct equivalent is `Application` class + `MainActivity`. The profile flag maps to a SharedPreferences read in `onCreate`. No complex logic here.

## 2. ContentView.swift

### Purpose
The root tab-bar shell. Renders a `TabView` with four tabs: Calendar, Holidays (Booked), Balance (Dashboard), and Settings. Forces re-renders on profile change by using `.id(activeProfile)` on profile-sensitive tabs.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None defined. Pure navigation shell.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Global dark/light mode toggle |
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Forces tab refresh on profile switch |

### Navigation Links / Routes
| Tab | View | Tag Icon |
|---|---|---|
| Calendar | `CalendarView` | `calendar` |
| Holidays | `BookedView` | `beach.umbrella.fill` |
| Balance | `BalanceView` | `chart.pie.fill` |
| Settings | `SettingsView` | `gearshape` |

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- `.preferredColorScheme` driven by `isDarkMode` — applied globally to all child views.
- `.id(activeProfile)` pattern forces full re-init of profile-scoped views when profile switches.

### UI Components Rendered
- `TabView` with 4 `tabItem` entries.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — Maps directly to a `BottomNavigationView` (or `NavigationBar` in Compose). Dark mode maps to `AppCompatDelegate.setDefaultNightMode`. Profile-based refresh maps to `ViewModel` state invalidation.

## 3. CalendarManager.swift

### Purpose
Singleton service that writes holiday events into the device's native calendar via the `EventKit` framework. Creates one `EKEvent` (all-day) per calendar day in the holiday range, with an emoji-prefixed title based on leave type.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- No custom models. Uses `EKEvent` from EventKit and the app's own `Holiday` struct as input.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Service layer only.

### External Dependencies
- `Foundation`
- `EventKit` — `EKEventStore`, `EKEvent`, `requestFullAccessToEvents`

### Special Features
- **System Calendar Integration:** Requests full calendar write access at runtime.
- Iterates day-by-day across a date range to create individual all-day events.
- Emoji mapping for leave types: Annual Leave 🏖️, Sick Leave 🤒, Personal 🏡, Allocated 📅.

### UI Components Rendered
- None. Pure data service.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — Android equivalent uses `ContentResolver` + `CalendarContract` provider. Requires `READ_CALENDAR` / `WRITE_CALENDAR` permissions in the manifest. The day-iteration logic ports directly. The emoji mapping is trivial.

---

## 4. CalendarView.swift

### Purpose
The most complex file in the project. This is the main calendar screen and contains multiple embedded sub-views and helper structures:
- **`DutyScanner`** (class) — Vision OCR engine that scans a photo and extracts duty number, sign-on, and sign-off times using regex.
- **`CalendarView`** (struct) — The primary SwiftUI View with Day/Week/Month swipeable calendar pickers, photo slots (up to 3 per day), note display, and quick-shift assignment.
- **`DetailArea`** — Shows parsed shift details (duty, sign-on/off), holiday flag, special day, and personal notes for the selected date.
- **`QuickShiftGrid`** — Two pages of 6 quick-assign shift-type buttons (Early, Late, OFF, etc.).
- **`SingleDayNoteSheet`** — Sheet for editing personal notes on a day.
- **`RestDayPatternView`** — Pattern generator that reads an existing shift sequence and projects it forward over a configurable number of weeks.
- **`AddPhotoButton`** — Photo slot component with camera, library, file, and "Search Duty Board" (PDF lookup) options.
- **`CalendarQuickShiftButton`** — Compact gradient button used in `QuickShiftGrid`.
- **`CameraPicker`** — `UIViewControllerRepresentable` wrapper for `UIImagePickerController` (camera source).
- **Global helper functions:** `decodeNotes`, `encodeNotes`, `getNoteForDate`, `extractShiftDetails`.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- `DutyScanner` — static class with `scanImage(uiImage:completion:)` and `matchPattern(for:in:)` helpers.
- Notes stored as `[String: String]` dictionary keyed by `Date.startOfDay.description`, JSON-encoded to a single `String`.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key (via KeyGen) | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dayNotesData_{profile}` | String | `""` | JSON dict of all day notes |
| `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` | String | `""` | JSON-encoded `[Holiday]` |
| `startOnMonday` | Bool | `true` | Calendar week start day |
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Dark/light colour scheme |
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Scopes file storage paths |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- `.sheet` → `SingleDayNoteSheet` (tap detail area)
- `.sheet` → `RestDayPatternView` (wand button in header)
- `.fullScreenCover` → `ZoomableImageView` (tap photo)

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`
- `PhotosUI` — `PhotosPickerItem`, `.photosPicker`
- `UIKit` — `UIImage`, `UIImagePickerController`, `UIGraphicsImageRenderer`
- `Vision` — `VNImageRequestHandler`, `VNRecognizeTextRequest`, `VNRecognizedTextObservation`

### Special Features
1. **OCR / Vision:** `DutyScanner` uses Apple Vision to extract duty number, sign-on and sign-off times from a photographed roster screen. Regex patterns handle varied formats (colons vs dots, "Sign On:" vs "On:", labelled vs unlabelled duty numbers).
2. **Photo Storage:** Up to 3 photos per day, stored as JPEG files in the app's Documents directory. File naming: `photo_{profile}_{dateTimestamp}_{index}.jpg`.
3. **Auto PDF Lookup:** When a photo is saved in Slot 0 (the roster scan), `DutyScanner` fires, extracts the duty number, then `PDFManager` is called to render the matching PDF page into Slot 1 automatically.
4. **Manual PDF Jump:** "Search Duty Board" option allows manually typing a duty number to render its PDF page into Slot 3.
5. **Pattern Generator (`RestDayPatternView`):** Reads shift keywords from existing notes over a configurable cycle length and reprojects them forward up to 104 weeks.
6. **Import/Export Data:** (via clipboard, handled in SettingsView, data read here).
7. **Three view modes:** Day view (single day + 3 photo slots), Week view (7-row list), Month view (grid).

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`
- `TabView` (paginated, used for month/week/day swiping)
- `LazyVGrid` (month grid)
- `ScrollView` with `DetailArea` + `QuickShiftGrid`
- `CalendarDayCell` (per grid cell)
- `AddPhotoButton` (3× per day)
- `ZoomableImageView` (full-screen cover)
- `RestDayPatternView` (sheet)
- `SingleDayNoteSheet` (sheet)
- `Slider`, `DatePicker`, `TextEditor`, `TextField`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**High** — Most complex file in the project. Key challenges:
- Vision OCR → Android ML Kit Text Recognition
- Photos (camera + gallery + files) → ActivityResultContracts with MediaStore/FileProvider
- File storage scheme → Room or raw file storage in `filesDir`
- Paginated swipe views (Day/Week/Month) → `ViewPager2` or Compose `HorizontalPager`
- PDF rendering → Android `PdfRenderer` API
- Pattern generator logic is pure algorithmic — ports cleanly

## 5. CalendarDayCell..swift

> **Note:** The filename contains a double dot (`CalendarDayCell..swift`), which is unusual but valid in the filesystem.

### Purpose
A single reusable cell view for the month calendar grid. Displays the day number, a background colour tinted by the shift type assigned to that day, and a priority-ordered icon slot (personal note 📝 > booked holiday emoji > bank holiday red dot).

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None defined. Stateless view consuming externally-provided data.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None (all data passed in via parameters).

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Exposes an `onTap: () -> Void` callback.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `Foundation`

### Special Features
- **Colour coding by shift type:** `getCalendarDutyColor(for:)` inspects the note string for keywords (Early, Middle, Late, Night, OFF, Day, Personal, Holiday, Training, Standby, Sickness) and returns a corresponding `Color`.
- **Personal note detection:** `hasPersonalNote(_:)` filters out shift keywords and scanner lines to determine if user has typed a custom note.
- **Bank holiday indicator:** Calls `Holiday.getSpecialDay(for:)` and shows a small red `Circle` if a UK bank holiday falls on that date.
- Selected state shown with a blue border, today shown with blue day number, bank holidays with red day number.

### UI Components Rendered
- `ZStack`, `VStack`, `Text`, `Circle`, `RoundedRectangle` (overlay border)

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — Maps to a custom `View` or Composable used inside a `LazyVerticalGrid`. The colour-coding logic is a simple string-match → colour lookup (trivially portable). The `Holiday.getSpecialDay` call requires the UK bank holiday logic be ported too.

## 6. Holiday.swift

### Purpose
The core data model for booked leave entries, plus a sophisticated UK bank holiday calculator.

**`Holiday` struct** — Codable, Identifiable model for a single leave entry (name, amount in days/hours, start date, optional end date, type string).

**`Holiday` extension (SMART HOLIDAY CALCULATOR):**
- `getSpecialDay(for:)` — Checks user-saved custom events first (`savedSpecialDaysData`), then falls back to the built-in UK calculator.
- `calculateUKHoliday(for:)` — Computes fixed dates (Christmas, New Year, Halloween etc.) and algorithmically calculated dates (Easter via anonymous Gregorian algorithm, Mother's Day, Father's Day, all UK bank holiday Mondays).
- Helpers: `isNthWeekday`, `isLastWeekday`, `getEasterSunday` (full anonymous Gregorian algorithm).
- `Weekday` enum with raw `Int` values mapping to `Calendar.weekday` component.

### Key Data Structures / Models
```swift
struct Holiday: Identifiable, Codable {
    var id: UUID
    var name: String
    var amount: Double      // days or hours
    var date: Date          // start date
    var endDate: Date?      // optional end date
    var type: String        // "Annual Leave" | "Sick Leave" | "Personal" | "Allocated" | "Lieu Day"
    var actualEndDate: Date { endDate ?? date }
}
```

Static encode/decode helpers using `JSONEncoder`/`JSONDecoder`.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `savedSpecialDaysData` | String (JSON) | User-defined custom special day overrides |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Model/logic layer only.

### External Dependencies
- `Foundation`

### Special Features
- **Full anonymous Gregorian Easter algorithm** — calculates Easter Sunday for any year.
- **UK Bank Holiday Calendar:** New Year's Day, Good Friday, Easter Sunday/Monday, Mother's Day (UK, 3 weeks before Easter), Early May Bank Holiday (1st Monday in May), Spring Bank Holiday (last Monday in May), Summer Bank Holiday (last Monday in August), Father's Day (3rd Sunday June), Remembrance Sunday (2nd Sunday November), plus fixed dates.
- Static JSON encode/decode factory methods for array serialisation.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — The data model is a trivial Kotlin data class. The UK holiday calculator logic is pure algorithmic and ports directly. JSON encode/decode maps to Gson/Moshi/kotlinx.serialization. The `UserDefaults` key for custom special days maps to SharedPreferences.

---

## 7. KeyGen.swift

### Purpose
Centralised namespace for generating profile-scoped `UserDefaults` / `AppStorage` keys. Prevents key string literals being scattered across the codebase.

### Key Data Structures / Models
```swift
struct KeyGen {
    static func notes(_ profile: String) -> String      // "dayNotesData_{profile}"
    static func holidays(_ profile: String) -> String   // "bookedHolidaysData_{profile}"
    static func annual(_ profile: String) -> String     // "annualAllowance_{profile}"
    static func hourly(_ profile: String) -> String     // "hourlyAllowance_{profile}"
    static func isHours(_ profile: String) -> String    // "isHoursMode_{profile}"
}
```

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys Generated
| Method | Generated Key Pattern |
|---|---|
| `KeyGen.notes(profile)` | `dayNotesData_{profile}` |
| `KeyGen.holidays(profile)` | `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` |
| `KeyGen.annual(profile)` | `annualAllowance_{profile}` |
| `KeyGen.hourly(profile)` | `hourlyAllowance_{profile}` |
| `KeyGen.isHours(profile)` | `isHoursMode_{profile}` |

Profile values in use: `"User1"`, `"User2"`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `Foundation`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — Direct equivalent is a Kotlin `object` (singleton) with the same static string-builder functions. Keys feed into SharedPreferences or a DataStore namespace.

---

## 8. OnboardingView.swift

### Purpose
First-run setup screen. Shown when `hasCompletedSetup` is `false`. Lets the user pick between days-based or hours-based tracking mode and set their annual leave allowance. Sets `hasCompletedSetup = true` on "Get Started" to dismiss onboarding permanently.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None. Pure UI form with `@AppStorage` bindings.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hasCompletedSetup` | Bool | `false` | One-time onboarding gate |
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Determines which profile's keys to write |
| `isHoursMode_{profile}` | Bool | `false` | Days vs hours tracking mode |
| `annualAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `25` | Annual leave days allowance |
| `hourlyAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `187.5` | Annual leave hours allowance |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- On save: sets `hasCompletedSetup = true` → parent (`SplashScreenView` / `ContentView`) transitions to main app.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- Dynamic default suggestion: switching to hours mode pre-fills `187.5` (standard UK annual hours), days mode pre-fills `25`.
- Allowance saved to the correct key (`hourlyAllowance` vs `annualAllowance`) based on mode selected.

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `VStack`, `Image` (SF Symbol), `Form`, `Section`, `Toggle`, `TextField`, `Button`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — A single `Fragment` or `Activity` with a simple form. SharedPreferences write on submit. The days/hours toggle logic is straightforward.

---

## 9. SplashScreenView.swift

### Purpose
Animated splash/launch screen. Shows the app logo (`AppLogo` asset) and tagline with a scale-and-fade-in animation, then transitions to `ContentView` after ~1 second.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None directly. Delegates routing logic to `ContentView` / `OnboardingView`.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- After `1.0s`: transitions to `ContentView()` via `isActive` state flag with `.opacity` transition animation.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- Custom `AppLogo` image asset (must be in `Assets.xcassets`).
- `AccentColor` color asset used as background.
- Scale (0.8 → 1.0) + opacity (0.5 → 1.0) animation on appear.

### UI Components Rendered
- `ZStack`, `Image("AppLogo")`, `Text`, `VStack`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — Android equivalent is a `SplashScreen` API (API 31+) or a dedicated splash `Activity`. The animation maps to `ObjectAnimator` or Compose `AnimatedVisibility`. `AppLogo` asset needs to be added to `res/drawable`.

## 10. SettingsView.swift

### Purpose
Settings screen with sections covering: profile management (2 profiles, renameable), appearance (dark mode, hours vs days), data share (clipboard export/import), duty board PDF management (upload/delete MF/SAT/SUN PDFs), push notifications (daily reminder), calendar configuration (week start), data management (reset options), and developer support (PayPal / Buy Me a Coffee links).

### Key Data Structures / Models
- `ResetType` enum — Identifiable, used to drive confirmation dialogs:
  - `.calendar`, `.booked`, `.allowance`, `.all`, `.export`, `.importSuccess`

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Active profile selector |
| `profileName_User1` | String | `"Profile 1"` | Display name for profile 1 |
| `profileName_User2` | String | `"Profile 2"` | Display name for profile 2 |
| `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` | String | `""` | Profile-scoped holidays JSON |
| `annualAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `0` | Annual days allowance |
| `hourlyAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `0` | Annual hours allowance |
| `dayNotesData_{profile}` | String | `""` | Calendar notes JSON |
| `isHoursMode_{profile}` | Bool | `false` | Days vs hours mode |
| `carriedOverAllowance` | Double | `0` | Carried-over leave days |
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Global dark mode |
| `startOnMonday` | Bool | `true` | Week start day |
| `notificationsEnabled` | Bool | `false` | Daily reminder on/off |
| `notificationTime` | Double | `32400` | Reminder time (epoch seconds) |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- `.fileImporter` for PDF import (MF/SAT/SUN).
- External `Link` to `https://paypal.me/MarkWillowApp` and `https://buymeacoffee.com/MarkWillow`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`
- `UserNotifications` — `UNUserNotificationCenter`, `UNMutableNotificationContent`, `UNCalendarNotificationTrigger`
- `UniformTypeIdentifiers` — `.pdf` content type for file importer

### Special Features
1. **PDF Management:** Upload MF (Mon-Fri), SAT, SUN duty board PDFs to the app Documents directory. Files stored as `{type}.pdf`. Status shown with green checkmark. Deletable with trash button.
2. **Clipboard Export/Import:** Serialises `dayNotesData` + `bookedHolidaysData` into a single delimited string (`DUTY_DATA:...###HOLIDAY_DATA:...`). Import parses the same format from clipboard.
3. **Daily Notification:** Schedules a repeating `UNCalendarNotificationTrigger` for the configured time. Cancels all pending notifications when disabled.
4. **Data Reset:** Four granular reset levels (allowances only, holidays only, calendar notes only, all data).

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `Form`, multiple `Section`s, `Picker` (segmented), `Toggle`, `DatePicker`, `TextField`, `Button`, `Label`, `Link`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**High** — Multiple system integrations to port:
- Notifications → `WorkManager` / `AlarmManager` + `NotificationChannel`
- File import → `ActivityResultContracts.OpenDocument` with PDF MIME type + `contentResolver` copy
- Clipboard → `ClipboardManager`
- Profile renaming + multi-profile SharedPreferences keys
- PDF file management in `filesDir`

## 11. BookedView.swift

### Purpose
The "Holidays" tab. Displays a searchable, sortable list of booked leave entries. Supports add, edit, and swipe-to-delete. The add/edit sheet includes description, amount (days or hours), leave type picker, and date range pickers. When a new holiday is saved, it is also written to the device calendar via `CalendarManager`.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- Uses `Holiday` struct (from `Holiday.swift`).
- Local state: `[Holiday]` array decoded from `bookedHolidaysData`.
- `categories` constant: `["Annual Leave", "Allocated", "Personal"]` — leave types shown in this view (Lieu and Sick are managed elsewhere).

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Profile context |
| `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` | String | `""` | JSON-encoded holidays array |
| `isHoursMode_{profile}` | Bool | `false` | Show "h" vs "d" suffix |
| `startOnMonday` | Bool | `true` | DatePicker week start |
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Text colour adjustments |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- `.sheet` → inline `Form` for add/edit (presented via `showingSheet` state).
- On save: calls `CalendarManager.shared.addHolidayToCalendar(holiday:endDate:)`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- **Dynamic units:** Displays amounts as `Xh` (hours) or `Xd` (days) based on profile's `isHoursMode`.
- **Auto end-date sync:** End date bumps to match start date if start date moves past it.
- **Search:** `.searchable` modifier filters by name or type.
- **Sort toggle:** Ascending/descending by start date.
- **EventKit integration:** Every new holiday entry is synchronised to the system calendar.

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `List` with `ForEach` + `.onDelete`, `Button`, `HStack`, `VStack`, `Form`, `Section`, `TextField`, `DatePicker`, `Picker` (wheel)

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — `RecyclerView` or Compose `LazyColumn` with swipe-to-delete. Add/edit maps to a `BottomSheetDialogFragment` or a dedicated screen. Calendar sync requires `CalendarContract` write. The date validation (end ≥ start) is trivial to port.

---

## 12. DashboardView.swift

### Purpose
The "Balance" tab. Named `DashboardView.swift` but contains `BalanceView` (the tab content) plus supporting sub-views. Displays the leave allowance balance as an animated circular progress ring, a breakdown (yearly allowance, carried over, lieu accrued), and a "days taken" counter. Sub-views:

- **`BalanceView`** — Main balance screen with circular chart.
- **`QuickLieuView`** — Sheet for adding/viewing lieu day credits.
- **`QuickAllowanceEditView`** — Mini sheet to edit a single numeric value (allowance or carried-over).
- **`StatBox`** — Reusable tappable stat card.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- Uses `Holiday` struct for lieu day records (type `"Lieu Day"`, negative `amount` = earned, positive = taken).

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Profile scoping |
| `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` | String | `""` | All holidays including lieu |
| `annualAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `0` | Days allowance |
| `hourlyAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `0` | Hours allowance |
| `carriedOverAllowance_{profile}` | Double | `0` | Carried-over days/hours |
| `isHoursMode_{profile}` | Bool | `false` | Units display |
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Appearance |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- `.sheet` → `QuickLieuView` (tap Lieu stat box)
- `.sheet` → `QuickAllowanceEditView` (tap Yearly or Carried Over stat box)

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- **Animated circular progress ring:** Uses `Circle().trim(from:to:)` with `StrokeStyle` and `rotationEffect`. Two pages (remaining vs used) in a `TabView` inside the circle.
- **Lieu calculation:** Computed property. Scans `bookedHolidaysData` for entries with type `"Lieu Day"` — negative amounts are credits (earned), positive are debits (taken). Net accrued shown as a balance.
- **Inline editable allowances:** Tap any `StatBox` to open a large-number `TextField` sheet.
- **`presentationDetents`:** `QuickAllowanceEditView` uses `.height(350)` detent for a partial sheet.

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `ScrollView`, `ZStack`, `Circle` (trimmed for chart), `TabView` (inside ring), `HStack`, `StatBox`, `Form`, `Stepper`, `List`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**High** — The circular progress chart requires custom drawing (`Canvas` API or a library like MPAndroidChart / Compose `drawArc`). The two-page swipe inside the chart is a unique UX pattern. Lieu tracking logic is pure arithmetic and ports easily. The `presentationDetents` (partial bottom sheet) maps to `BottomSheetBehavior` with `peekHeight`.

---

## 13. DutyIntents.swift

### Purpose
Siri Shortcuts integration via the `AppIntents` framework. Provides two App Intents that allow users to ask Siri for their duty details:

- **`GetDutyIntent`** — Reads today's duty from `UserDefaults` and returns a spoken/displayed string.
- **`GetTomorrowDutyIntent`** — Same but for tomorrow.
- **`MyWorkDiaryShortcuts`** — `AppShortcutsProvider` that registers natural language phrases for both intents.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None beyond `AppIntent` conformances.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `dayNotesData` | String | **Hardcoded key** (not profile-scoped via KeyGen) — potential bug for User2 |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Siri/Shortcuts surface.

### External Dependencies
- `AppIntents` — `AppIntent`, `IntentResult`, `ReturnsValue`, `ProvidesDialog`, `IntentDialog`, `AppShortcut`, `AppShortcutsProvider`
- `Foundation`
- Indirectly uses `decodeNotes` and `extractShiftDetails` from `CalendarView.swift`.

### Special Features
- **Siri Integration:** Exposes voice phrases like "What's my duty in My Work Diary", "Check tomorrow's duty in My Work Diary".
- **`@MainActor` bridging:** Wraps calls to `decodeNotes` and `extractShiftDetails` in `await MainActor.run { }` to safely call UI-thread helpers from async context.
- Detects "OFF" day and returns appropriate spoken response.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**High** — Android has no direct `AppIntents` equivalent. Options:
  1. **App Actions** (Google Assistant) via `shortcuts.xml` + in-app fulfillment handlers — closest equivalent.
  2. **Slices API** (deprecated).
  3. **Widgets** — a simpler alternative for quick glance data.
  The logic (read SharedPreferences → parse note → compose string) is trivial; the integration surface is the challenge. **Note the hardcoded key bug** — should use `KeyGen.notes(activeProfile)`.

---

## 14. PDFManager.swift

### Purpose
Singleton service that searches uploaded duty board PDFs for a given duty number and renders the matching page as a `UIImage`.

Logic:
1. Determines which PDF to search (MF/SAT/SUN) based on the day of the week of the given date.
2. Opens the PDF from the Documents directory.
3. Iterates pages, reads the first 10 lines of each page's text content.
4. Returns the first page whose header starts with the given duty number.
5. Renders the page to a `UIImage` using `UIGraphicsImageRenderer`.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None. Stateless service (singleton pattern).

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None. Files read from `FileManager.default.urls(.documentDirectory)`.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Service layer.

### External Dependencies
- `UIKit` — `UIImage`, `UIGraphicsImageRenderer`, `UIColor`
- `PDFKit` — `PDFDocument`, `PDFPage`

### Special Features
- **PDF text extraction:** Uses `PDFPage.string` (PDFKit) to get text content per page for duty number matching.
- **Day-of-week routing:** Automatically selects `MF.pdf`, `SAT.pdf`, or `SUN.pdf` based on the date's weekday.
- **Suffix stripping:** Strips any suffix from duty numbers (e.g., "53.a" → "53") before searching.
- **Page rendering:** Draws PDF page to a CGContext with white background fill, Y-axis flip for correct orientation.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**High** — Android's `PdfRenderer` API (API 21+) renders pages to `Bitmap` objects but does **not** provide text extraction. Text search requires either:
  - A third-party library (e.g., `iTextPDF`, `Apache PDFBox`, `PdfiumAndroid`) for text extraction.
  - Or OCR on the rendered bitmap (ML Kit).
  Page rendering to `Bitmap` is available natively. Day-of-week routing logic is trivial.

---

## 15. ShiftButton.swift

### Purpose
A reusable full-width shift assignment button with an icon (SF Symbol) and label, rendered with a semi-transparent tinted background and coloured border. Used in older/alternate shift-assignment UI contexts (a more expanded button style vs `QuickShiftButton`).

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None. Pure UI component, no state.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Exposes `action: () -> Void` callback.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- Accessible: `.accessibilityLabel("\(label) button")`.
- Preview included with sample shift grid.

### UI Components Rendered
- `Button`, `HStack`, `Image` (SF Symbol), `Text`, `RoundedRectangle` (background + stroke overlay)

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — A Compose `Button` or custom styled `MaterialButton` with icon. The background + border style maps to `OutlinedButton` or custom `shape`/`border` in Compose.

---

## 16. QuickShiftButton.swift

### Purpose
A compact vertically-stacked quick-assign button (icon above label) with a solid gradient-coloured background and drop shadow. Used inside `QuickShiftGrid` cells. Slightly different visual style from `ShiftButton` — more compact, solid fill vs tinted outline.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None. Stateless component.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | `true` | Read but **not actually used** in rendering — vestigial dependency |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Exposes `action: () -> Void`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`

### Special Features
- Drop shadow with colour-matched opacity.
- Note: `isDarkMode` `@AppStorage` is declared but not referenced in the body — dead code.

### UI Components Rendered
- `Button`, `VStack`, `Image` (SF Symbol), `Text`

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — Compose `Button` with `containerColor`, icon, and `shadow` modifier. The `isDarkMode` dead code can simply be removed.

---

## 17. SingleDayNoteEditor.swift

### Purpose
A standalone note editor view for a single calendar day. Appears to be an earlier/simpler version of the note editing functionality that is also present inline within `CalendarView.swift` (as `SingleDayNoteSheet`). Provides a `TextEditor` with save, cancel, and delete (destructive) toolbar actions.

**Important:** This view is a near-duplicate of `SingleDayNoteSheet` in `CalendarView.swift`. The `CalendarView.swift` version is the one actually used in the live app (it includes `QuickShiftGrid`). This file may be legacy/unused — verify usage at Android conversion time.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- None. Operates on `[String: String]` note dict via local encode/decode helpers (duplicated from global helpers in `CalendarView.swift`).

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `activeProfile` | String | `"User1"` | Declared but not used in logic — vestigial |

### Navigation Links / Routes
- Dismiss via `@Environment(\.dismiss)` on save, cancel, or delete.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `Foundation`

### Special Features
- **Note:** Contains its own private `decode`/`encode` functions — duplicates global helpers.
- The `activeProfile` `@AppStorage` property is declared but never used in any function — dead code.
- Delete removes the note entirely (no shift-type preservation logic, unlike the `SingleDayNoteSheet` version).

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `VStack`, `Text` (date title), `TextEditor`, toolbar `Button`s (Cancel, Save, Delete)

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — If confirmed as legacy/unused, may not need porting. If ported: a `Fragment`/Composable with a `TextField` (multiline), toolbar actions, and SharedPreferences read/write.

---

## 18. ZoomItem.swift

### Purpose
A minimal data-transfer object (DTO) used to pass a photo and its metadata into `ZoomableImageView` when a user taps a photo in the Day view.

### Key Data Structures / Models
```swift
struct ZoomItem: Identifiable {
    let id: UUID          // auto-generated, for List/sheet identification
    let image: UIImage    // the tapped photo
    let date: Date        // the calendar date this photo belongs to
    let index: Int        // which slot (0, 1, or 2) was tapped
}
```

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- Used as the `item` in `.fullScreenCover(item: $selectedZoomItem)` in `CalendarView`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `UIKit`

### Special Features
- None beyond being `Identifiable` to work with SwiftUI's sheet/cover presentation.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Low** — A Kotlin `data class` or a simple `Parcelable` passed via navigation arguments or a `ViewModel`.

---

## 19. ZoomableImageView.swift

### Purpose
Full-screen photo viewer launched as a `fullScreenCover`. Wraps `ZoomableScrollView` inside a `TabView` (paged) to allow swiping between all 3 photo slots for a given date. Shows a "Delete Photo" button at the bottom. The initial page shown is the index of the photo that was tapped.

### Key Data Structures / Models
- Accepts `ZoomItem` (the tapped item), `[UIImage?]` (all 3 slots), and `onDelete: (Int) -> Void` callback.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- Dismissed via `@Environment(\.dismiss)` — "Close" toolbar button or post-delete.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `UIKit`

### Special Features
- **Swipeable slots:** `TabView` with `.page` style lets user swipe between Slot 1, 2, 3.
- **Empty slot placeholder:** Shows a SF Symbol + text if a slot has no photo.
- **Confirmation dialog before delete.**
- Passes `currentIndex` (not the original `item.index`) to `onDelete` — correctly deletes the photo currently visible, not necessarily the one originally tapped.

### UI Components Rendered
- `NavigationStack`, `ZStack`, `TabView` (paged), `ZoomableScrollView`, `Button`, `.confirmationDialog`, `.ultraThinMaterial` background

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — Full-screen `Fragment`/Activity with `ViewPager2` for swipe between slots. Each page hosts a `PhotoView` (third-party library) or a custom `Matrix`-based zoom `ImageView`. Delete confirmation maps to `AlertDialog`. The "current page index" tracking maps to `ViewPager2.currentItem`.

---

## 20. ZoomableScrollView.swift

### Purpose
Low-level pinch-to-zoom image view implemented by wrapping `UIScrollView` via `UIViewRepresentable`. Handles:
- Initial zoom set to "fit" the image in the container.
- Pinch-to-zoom (min 1×/fit scale, max 5× or 5× min scale).
- Centering the image when smaller than the container (via `contentInset`).
- **Double-tap to zoom in** (to 2× min scale, centred on tap point) or zoom out (back to fit).

### Key Data Structures / Models
- `Representable` — the `UIViewRepresentable` implementation.
- `Coordinator` — `UIScrollViewDelegate` that manages zooming, centering, and double-tap gesture.

### UserDefaults / AppStorage Keys
- None.

### Navigation Links / Routes
- None. Used inside `ZoomableImageView`.

### External Dependencies
- `SwiftUI`, `UIKit`

### Special Features
- **`UIScrollView`-based zoom** (not SwiftUI's `.scaleEffect`): delivers smooth, native-feeling pinch zoom with proper rubber-banding.
- **`viewForZooming(in:)`** returns the `UIImageView` to enable native UIScrollView zoom.
- **Double-tap gesture** calculates the tap-centred zoom rect for `scrollView.zoom(to:rect:animated:)`.
- `didSetInitialZoom` flag prevents re-setting initial zoom on layout re-renders.

### UI Components Rendered
- `UIScrollView` + `UIImageView` (wrapped). Exposed as SwiftUI `View`.

### Android Conversion Complexity
**Medium** — Android has no native equivalent this smooth. Options:
  1. **`PhotoView` library** (recommended) — drop-in `ImageView` with pinch zoom and double-tap. Minimal effort.
  2. **Custom `Matrix`-based `ImageView`** — full manual implementation using `ScaleGestureDetector` and `GestureDetector`.
  In Compose: `Modifier.transformable` + manual state tracking, or a community library like `zoomable`.

---

## Cross-Cutting Summary

### All UserDefaults Keys (Consolidated)

| Key | Type | Scope | Used In |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hasCompletedSetup` | Bool | Global | App entry, Onboarding |
| `activeProfile` | String | Global | All profile-scoped views |
| `profileName_User1` | String | Global | Settings |
| `profileName_User2` | String | Global | Settings |
| `isDarkMode` | Bool | Global | ContentView, CalendarView, Settings, BookedView, CalendarDayCell, QuickShiftButton |
| `startOnMonday` | Bool | Global | CalendarView, BookedView, Settings |
| `carriedOverAllowance` | Double | Global (not profile-scoped — potential bug) | Settings, Balance |
| `notificationsEnabled` | Bool | Global | Settings |
| `notificationTime` | Double | Global | Settings |
| `savedSpecialDaysData` | String | Global | Holiday.swift (custom special days override) |
| `dayNotesData_{profile}` | String | Per-profile | CalendarView, Settings |
| `bookedHolidaysData_{profile}` | String | Per-profile | CalendarView, BookedView, BalanceView, Settings |
| `annualAllowance_{profile}` | Double | Per-profile | Onboarding, BalanceView, Settings |
| `hourlyAllowance_{profile}` | Double | Per-profile | Onboarding, BalanceView, Settings |
| `isHoursMode_{profile}` | Bool | Per-profile | Onboarding, BookedView, BalanceView, Settings |
| `carriedOverAllowance_{profile}` | Double | Per-profile | BalanceView (note: Settings uses non-scoped key) |
| `dayNotesData` | String | Global (hardcoded) | DutyIntents.swift — **BUG: not profile-scoped** |

### File Storage (Beyond UserDefaults)

| File Pattern | Location | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `photo_{profile}_{timestamp}_{index}.jpg` | Documents dir | Per-day photos (3 slots) |
| `MF.pdf` | Documents dir | Mon-Fri duty board PDF |
| `SAT.pdf` | Documents dir | Saturday duty board PDF |
| `SUN.pdf` | Documents dir | Sunday duty board PDF |

### Shift Type Keywords
The following strings are used as note content identifiers across CalendarView, CalendarDayCell, QuickShiftGrid, and RestDayPatternView:
`Early`, `Middle`, `Late`, `Night`, `OFF`, `Day`, `Personal`, `Holiday`, `Training`, `Standby`, `Sickness`

### Leave Types
`Annual Leave`, `Allocated`, `Personal`, `Lieu Day`, `Sick Leave`

### Complexity Overview (Android Conversion)

| File | Complexity | Primary Challenge |
|---|---|---|
| AnnualLeaveTrackerApp | Low | Trivial app entry point |
| ContentView | Low | TabBar navigation |
| CalendarManager | Medium | CalendarContract permissions |
| **CalendarView** | **High** | OCR + photos + PDF + patterns + 3 view modes |
| CalendarDayCell | Medium | Custom cell with colour coding |
| Holiday | Medium | UK bank holiday algorithm |
| KeyGen | Low | Kotlin object equivalent |
| OnboardingView | Low | Simple form |
| SplashScreenView | Low | Splash screen animation |
| SettingsView | High | Notifications + PDF management + clipboard |
| BookedView | Medium | List + CRUD + calendar sync |
| **DashboardView** | **High** | Custom circular chart + lieu logic |
| **DutyIntents** | **High** | Google App Actions integration |
| **PDFManager** | **High** | PDF text search (no native Android API) |
| ShiftButton | Low | Styled button component |
| QuickShiftButton | Low | Styled button component |
| SingleDayNoteEditor | Low | May be legacy/unused |
| ZoomItem | Low | Data transfer object |
| ZoomableImageView | Medium | Paged photo viewer |
| ZoomableScrollView | Medium | Pinch-to-zoom (use PhotoView library) |
